commit.message:
feat(agents): add AI agents with LangChain.js and HOL compliance

- PlayDetectionAgent: polls Spotify/Last.fm, resolves ISRCs via MusicBrainz,
  publishes verified play events to HCS PLAY_EVENTS_TOPIC
- DistributionAgent: subscribes via HCS-10, checks disputes, looks up on-chain
  splits, triggers RoyaltyVault.distribute(), confirms payments on HCS
- DisputeResolutionAgent: monitors DISPUTES_TOPIC, cross-references ISRC
  ownership via MusicBrainz, auto-resolves or escalates
- ArtistAdvisorAgent: primary user-facing agent with ConversationSummaryBuffer
  Memory; handles registration, earnings history, split management, dispute filing
- HOLRegistryClient: registers all four agents using @hashgraphonline/standards-sdk
  (HCS-11 profile) with deterministic fallback DIDs
- A2AMessageBus: HCS-10 inter-agent messaging for all four A2A flows

commit.message:
Rewrite JellyseerrService as comprehensive Jellyseerr/Overseerr API client

Bug fixes:
- Remove 'using' on HttpClient from IHttpClientFactory (prevents socket exhaustion)
- Fix status code 2 (pending) falling through to 'not_requested' via MapStatus()
- Add URL validation (http/https only) to prevent SSRF attacks
- Validate serviceType (radarr/sonarr) and mediaType (movie/tv) parameters

New features:
- 4K support via Is4k flag on RequestPayload
- Root folder selection via GetRootFoldersAsync()
- Language profiles via GetLanguageProfilesAsync() (Sonarr only)
- Tags via GetTagsAsync()
- Season-specific requests with int[] instead of object
- Server info via GetServiceServersAsync()
- Request quota via GetRequestCountAsync()
- Media lookup via GetMediaInfoAsync()
- Full model classes for all API responses

Safety improvements:
- SSRF prevention with URL scheme validation
- CancellationToken and ConfigureAwait(false) on all async paths
- Comprehensive try/catch with structured logging
- XML doc comments on all public members
- Input sanitization on all URL-interpolated parameters

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement real-time process lifecycle monitoring via eBPF (#53)

* feat(probes): implement real-time process lifecycle monitoring via eBPF

- Life-cycle tracking: Monitor the full lifespan of a process from creation
  (exec_watch) to destruction (do_group_exit).
- Precise longevity: Capture the exact duration of short-lived processes in
  milliseconds by synchronizing timestamps between exec and exit.
- Contextual linkage: Track cgroup_id to map process lifecycles to specific
  container workloads in Kubernetes/AI training environments.
- Death cause analysis: Enhance exit observability by distinguishing
  between MANUAL_SIGKILL and OOM_TERMINATED through kernel-level victim tagging.
- Reliability: Resolve 'bash' context races and kernel-version dependency
  by implementing name caching and Kprobe-based exit interception.

* lint

* allow dead code in vmlinux structs for CI

* feat: Add process lifecycle monitoring with eBPF

- Implement full-path lifecycle tracking (exec → exit)
- Add intelligent OOM vs manual kill discrimination
- Resolve race conditions with process name caching
- Capture precise process duration metrics
- Include cgroup_id for Kubernetes Pod correlation
- Update Helm chart to support process_lifecycle probe

* Apply suggestions from code review

* refactor: Remove unused exit_signal field from ProcessExitEvent

- Remove exit_signal field to prevent uninitialized memory leaks

* fix(process_lifecycle): fix OOM victim PID extraction and ppid marker

- fix: ctx.arg::<u32>(0) was reading oom_control* pointer as raw u32 (garbage value)
  now follows oom_control.chosen -> task_struct.tgid pointer chain via bpf_probe_read_kernel
- fix: OOM victim was setting ppid=0 instead of 0xDEAD, causing userspace to always
  classify OOM kills as MANUAL_SIGKILL
- add: oom_control struct definition to vmlinux.rs

* style: fix rustfmt alignment in oom_control struct

* fix: use correct aya bpf_probe_read_kernel API signature

commit.message:
fix: resolve absolute path for otel-hook in setup.sh (#36)

When otel-hook is on the shell PATH, setup.sh now writes the fully
resolved absolute path (e.g. /usr/local/bin/otel-hook) into hooks.json
instead of the bare command name. This ensures IDEs like Cursor that
don't inherit the shell PATH can still find the binary.

Changes:
- setup.sh: Use $(command -v otel-hook) to resolve absolute path
- setup.sh: Update LOG_HOME check to match absolute path pattern
- tests: Update assertion to expect absolute path

commit.message:
Fix status.sh to properly detect background services with whitespace

The check_pid function was not trimming whitespace from service names,
causing it to look for .pid files with trailing spaces. This prevented
detection of iot-ingestion, alert-service, and analytics-beat.

Now uses $(echo "$1" | xargs) to trim whitespace before constructing
the .pid file path. All background services now properly detected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Claude/opencode hooks plugin support (#31)

* feat: add OpenCode plugin support

Adds a native TypeScript plugin (plugin/opencode.ts) that integrates
otel-hook with OpenCode's plugin system, following the same thin-wrapper
pattern as rtk.

- plugin/opencode.ts: exports OtelHookPlugin; checks otel-hook is in
  PATH at load time, then hooks session.created/deleted → SessionStart/
  SessionEnd and tool.execute.before/after → PreToolUse/PostToolUse,
  piping JSON payloads to otel-hook via stdin with IDE_OTEL_IDE_NAME=opencode

- setup.sh: adds --opencode flag and setup_opencode() function that
  copies the plugin to .opencode/plugins/ (project) or
  ~/.config/opencode/plugins/ (--global); auto-detects OpenCode via
  command -v opencode or ~/.config/opencode; respects OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR

- examples/opencode-plugin.example.ts: annotated copy for users who
  want to customise the plugin

- README.md: replaces the placeholder OpenCode blurb with a proper
  setup section including quick-start commands and manual install steps

- tests/test_otel_hook.py: adds TestOpenCodePluginPayloads (11 tests)
  covering IDE detection, event normalisation, and priority ordering for
  all four payload types emitted by the plugin

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(opencode): handle all observable hook events

Extends plugin/opencode.ts (and the matching example) to cover the full
set of OpenCode events that map to canonical otel-hook events:

  session.idle    → Stop            (agent finished responding)
  message.updated → UserPromptSubmit (role=user messages only)
  session.error   → SessionEnd with status=error (closes trace on failure)

The previous commit only wired session.created/deleted and the tool
execute lifecycle. The three new branches follow the same thin-wrapper
pattern: build a minimal JSON payload and pipe it to otel-hook via
IDE_OTEL_IDE_NAME=opencode.

Tests: adds 8 new assertions to TestOpenCodePluginPayloads covering
IDE detection and event-name extraction for every payload shape the
updated plugin emits. All 185 tests pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

- Splits "Claude Code / Antigravity / compatible runners" into two
  separate columns: one for Claude Code / Antigravity, one for
  OpenCode (plugin) showing the native OpenCode event names that
  each canonical event maps from (session.created, session.idle,
  message.updated, tool.execute.before/after, etc.)
- Updates the "Events captured" blurb under the OpenCode setup

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

setup.sh --cursor always wrote to the project-level .cursor/hooks.json;

commit.message:
Fix KV cache context bleed in chat command

mlx_lm.server uses a trie-based prefix cache shared across ALL requests.
Single-turn messages share the '<|im_start|>user\n' token prefix, so a
new chat request could match a cached prefix from an unrelated earlier
request and inherit its context.

Fix: prepend a unique system message to every chat request so the token
sequence prefix is always different and never hits the shared cache.
Only affects 'mlx-server chat' — interactive REPL and direct API calls
are intentionally unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design
